successCriteria.description: File `src/modules/ping/ping.routes.ts` exists and exports an async function `pingRoutes(fastify: FastifyInstance): Promise<void>`
The file imports `FastifyInstance` from `fastify` and `PingService` from `./ping.service`

Registers `fastify.get('/ping', async (request, reply) => {...})` — the handler instantiates `PingService`, calls `getPing()`, and returns `reply.status(200).send(status)` where `status` is the `PingResponse` object `{ status: 'ok' }`

The handler body is wrapped in try/catch: on error, logs via `request.log.error(error)` and returns `reply.status(500).send({ error: 'Internal Server Error' })` — matching the uptime module pattern exactly

constraints: Must follow the exact structural pattern of `src/modules/uptime/uptime.routes.ts`: async function export, `fastify.get(...)` registration, handler instantiates service inline, try/catch with `request.log.error(error)` and 500 response `{ error: 'Internal Server Error' }`

Must import `PingService` (class, not interface) from `./ping.service` — confirmed by Phase 2 artifact at `src/modules/ping/ping.service.ts` which exports `class PingService implements IPingService` with method `getPing(): PingResponse` returning `{ status: 'ok' }`
Route path must be `/ping` (not `/uptime` or any other path)
Exported function must be named `pingRoutes` (matching the naming convention: module name + 'Routes')
outOfScope: Phase 4 — Create ping module barrel export (`src/modules/ping/index.ts`): re-export PingResponse, IPingService, PingService, pingRoutes
Phase 5 — Register ping routes in `src/app.ts`: import `pingRoutes` and call `app.register(pingRoutes)`
